Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. TruffleRuby 162 upvotes, 21 comments
  2. 6o6 v1.1: Faster 6502-on-6502 virtualization for a C64/Apple II Apple-1 emulator 27 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. TinyLoRA – Learning to Reason in 13 Parameters 209 upvotes, 34 comments
  4. MiniStack (replacement for LocalStack) 266 upvotes, 53 comments
  5. CERN levels up with new superconducting karts 263 upvotes, 54 comments
  6. Chess in SQL 121 upvotes, 24 comments
  7. 4D Doom 247 upvotes, 60 comments
  8. Bring Back MiniDV with This Raspberry Pi FireWire Hat 88 upvotes, 17 comments
  9. A dot a day keeps the clutter away 436 upvotes, 128 comments
  10. OpenAI Demand Sinks on Secondary Market as Anthropic Runs Hot 43 upvotes, 8 comments
  11. Open source CAD in the browser (Solvespace) 355 upvotes, 112 comments
  12. Claude Code Unpacked : A visual guide 783 upvotes, 282 comments
  13. Consider the Greenland Shark (2020) 33 upvotes, 6 comments
  14. Intuiting Pratt Parsing 82 upvotes, 22 comments
  15. Axios compromised on NPM – Malicious versions drop remote access trojan 1886 upvotes, 761 comments
  16. The Claude Code Source Leak: fake tools, frustration regexes, undercover mode 1279 upvotes, 520 comments
  17. New Patches Allow Building Linux IPv6-Only, Option to Deprecate "Legacy" IPv4 46 upvotes, 12 comments
  18. Show HN: 1-Bit Bonsai, the First Commercially Viable 1-Bit LLMs 330 upvotes, 132 comments
  19. Claude Wrote a Full FreeBSD Remote Kernel RCE with Root Shell (CVE-2026-4747) 141 upvotes, 51 comments
  20. Show HN: CLI to order groceries via reverse-engineered REWE API (Haskell) 150 upvotes, 58 comments
  21. Is BGP Safe Yet? No. Test Your ISP 90 upvotes, 36 comments
  22. AI has suddenly become more useful to open-source developers 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  23. Age verification now required for DNS resolution 27 upvotes, 11 comments
  24. Neanderthals survived on a knife's edge for 350k years 196 upvotes, 147 comments
  25. OpenAI closes funding round at an $852B valuation 490 upvotes, 448 comments
  26. Show HN: Sycamore – next gen Rust web UI library using fine-grained reactivity 55 upvotes, 38 comments
  27. I Quit. The Clankers Won 260 upvotes, 265 comments
  28. Show HN: Baton – A desktop app for developing with AI agents 38 upvotes, 32 comments
  29. Slop is not necessarily the future 279 upvotes, 450 comments
